title: Climate Risk, Adaptation, and Resilience
gist: This is a BCG climate-resilience practice landing page rather than an analytical article—mostly a catalog of public/private-sector offerings and client cases. Genuine signals: the thesis that physical climate risk translates to EBITDA-level financial loss, an adaptation-and-resilience financing gap, and the data point that only ~20% of country governments are planning necessary adaptation actions.

insights.claim: BCG frames physical climate risks such as extreme heat and flooding as translating directly into operational disruption and financial loss down to the EBITDA level, urging firms to map exposure and sequence adaptation by risk severity and business value.
insights.speaker: BCG Henderson Institute
insights.so_what: Pushes climate risk from disclosure box-ticking to a quantifiable earnings driver—relevant to asset-level and EBITDA-at-risk analysis.
insights.quote: Assess how risk translates to operational disruption and financial loss down to EBITDA effects.

insights.claim: BCG highlights an adaptation-and-resilience financing gap requiring a fundamental transformation of the financial system, and notes only roughly 20% of country governments are planning the necessary adaptation and resilience actions.
insights.speaker: BCG Henderson Institute
insights.so_what: A large unmet adaptation-finance need plus low government planning signals a structural opportunity in blended/resilience-infrastructure finance.
insights.quote: Only ~20% of country governments are planning the necessary A&R actions.
insights.locator: 2030 Adaptation Outcomes for Adaptation and Resilience Planning

insights.claim: BCG argues (via a linked report) that the power of adaptation strategies diminishes rapidly once global warming exceeds certain thresholds, so mitigation and adaptation must work in tandem.
insights.speaker: BCG Henderson Institute
insights.so_what: Caps the standalone value of adaptation investment absent mitigation—relevant to long-horizon climate-resilience asset pricing.
insights.quote: The power of adaptation strategies diminishes rapidly when global warming exceeds certain thresholds.
insights.locator: Why Climate Mitigation and Adaptation Must Work in Tandem
